original_reasoning: Arabic bury/crush/level can plausibly drift into causing damage or wrong; the link is moderate, not direct.
دحا as spread/open out plausibly reaches apartness or being in two sides.
الهزال can align with bedridden clinical weakness; this is a plausible drift from emaciation to the bed-bound patient context.
Arabic 'calamities' can meet Greek 'were being destroyed/perishing' through the ruin outcome.
Arabic gloss centers on difference, irregularity, or things jutting apart; Greek αἶσχος is shame/ugliness, a plausible abstract result of visible distortion.

The cord sense gives a plausible chain of binding or fastening that can abstract toward making people live together.
Plausible social-harm chain: Arabic has a calamitous or harmful matter; Greek is mob rule.
